binding_constraint_check.stage_1b_status: PARTIAL (TESTED for the package; cross-cutting wiring incremental)
binding_constraint_check.rationale: Same as Stage 1a. Stage 1b's admin handlers don't introduce new egress points (they use writeJSON + writeError which serialize structured payloads, not raw user input). Verification pass G7 still pending — should land before any Stage 2 OAuth/key code.
binding_constraint_check.incremental_acceptance: Until cross-cutting wiring lands, treat new auth-touching code as required to demonstrate redact integration in PR review.

TN2/TN4/TN5 not exercised yet; preserved without modification.

gaps.issue: T4 is `challenger: assumption` — perf budget (≤5ms cached / ≤50ms cold) was a guess in m1.
gaps.action: Before Stage 2 cache lands, run a microbench against the actual key/principal lookup path INCLUDING the event-driven invalidation overhead from RT-4. Either confirm or relax T4.

gaps.issue: RT-6 (binding) is at TESTED for the redact package itself, but cross-cutting wiring is incremental: every error response writer, every panic-recover middleware, every audit body writer needs to be inspected and confirmed to route through redact.
gaps.action: Audit pass before Stage 2: grep for slog.Error / writeError / http.Error / panic recovery and confirm each call site uses slog.Default() (which now redacts) or wraps user-controlled values in redact.String.

gaps.issue: JWKS cache (24h TTL, IdP-outage tolerance) deferred to Stage 1c per user scope choice. Projection layer SATISFIED; verify path is currently HMAC-stub-only.
gaps.action: Stage 1c: implement RS256 verifier + JWKS cache with 24h TTL + outage-tolerance fallback. Tests against fixture JWKS.

convergence.next_stage_suggestion: Stage 1c (Svelte admin pages + JWKS cache) OR Stage 2 (audit middleware + key minting). Recommended: Stage 1c since it closes RT-9 (frontend) and RT-5 (JWKS) in one focused frontend+verifier pass.
convergence.reason: After 2 iterations, 4 RTs SATISFIED (RT-5, RT-8, RT-12, RT-13), 5 PARTIAL (RT-3 schema, RT-6 binding-incremental, RT-7 per-IP only, RT-9 server-only, RT-10 schema), 5 NOT_SATISFIED (RT-1, RT-2, RT-4, RT-11, RT-14). Binding constraint RT-6 not fully closed (cross-cutting wiring incremental). 14 of 24 constraints SATISFIED or PARTIAL with implementation in place; 10 deferred-by-design.
